signals.raw:
Trump announces two-week ceasefire with Iran ahead of 8 p.m. ET deadline on April 7, 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
President Donald Trump confirmed Tuesday evening (April 7, 2026) that the U.S. reached a **two-week ceasefire agreement** with Iran, averting escalation over the Strait of Hormuz closure just before an 8 p.m. ET deadline.[1]

signals.headline: South Korea's inflation rises above target as BOK holds rates steady amid Middle East tensions | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
South Korea's inflation rises above target as BOK holds rates steady amid Middle East tensions | YES | HIGH confidence**

The most recent confirmed development is that **South Korea's annual inflation reached 2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in the previous two months, exceeding the Bank of Korea's 2% target.[2] This comes after the **BOK held its benchmark interest rate at 2.5% on April 10, 202

signals.headline: **Inflation acceleration**: The March reading of 2
signals.raw: **Inflation acceleration**: The March reading of 2.2% represents a breach of the central bank's target and signals renewed price pressures, particularly from rising global oil prices following the Iran conflict.[2][4]

signals.headline: **Currency weakness amplifying inflation risk**: The Korean won has weakened significantly to around 1,520 won per U
signals.raw: **Currency weakness amplifying inflation risk**: The Korean won has weakened significantly to around 1,520 won per U.S. dollar, which is expected to amplify imported inflation pressures.[4][6]

signals.headline: **BOK's hawkish hold**: Rather than cutting rates as some might have expected given moderating inflation earlier this ye
signals.raw: **BOK's hawkish hold**: Rather than cutting rates as some might have expected given moderating inflation earlier this year, the central bank has signaled an extended pause, with policymakers removing previous language hinting at further easing.[2] The BOK expects inflation to rise to the "mid to upper two-percent range" going forward.[4]
